title: Cost Analysis: If a company produces 50000 units and has fixed costs...
summary: Calculate fixed cost per unit by dividing total fixed costs by total units produced.
prompt: If a company produces 50,000 units and has fixed costs of $500,000, what is the fixed cost per unit?
solution: Fixed Cost per Unit = Total Fixed Costs / Total Units Produced = $500,000 / 50,000 = $10 per unit. This is crucial information for cost analysis (Page 51).
